title: Accessible Names, Images, Language, Headings, Landmarks, and Human Review · Runtime Lens: Automated Accessibility Limit
summary: The tool counts alt attributes, assumes heading rank alone proves structure, ignores accessible names and language, and labels automated checks WCAG conformance. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the automated accessibility limit increment in accessible page-quality audit with human-review handoff. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Map each automated finding to testable evidence, confidence, false-positive risk, required manual checks, and no-conformance claim.
Collect link and control text, labels, ARIA naming sources, context, duplicate names, target purpose, and keyboard-relevant markup for review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-accessibility-audit-2
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-accessibility-audit-2.
# Case 7417: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def worked_scraper_accessibility_audit_2(pages: int = 24, automated_findings: int = 38, manual_checks: int = 42, conformance_claims: int = 0, findings_with_evidence: int = 38) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "authorizationworked2",
        "url7417",
        "response2dekrow",
        "treeworked27417",
        "frontier7417worked2",
        "report2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or automated_findings < 0:
        return False, pages, "accessibility-count"
    if findings_with_evidence != automated_findings:
        return False, findings_with_evidence, "finding-evidence"
    if manual_checks < pages:
        return False, manual_checks, "manual-review-gap"
    if conformance_claims > 0:
        return False, conformance_claims, "conformance-overclaim"
    assert findings_with_evidence == automated_findings and conformance_claims == 0
    return True, automated_findings, "bounded-accessibility-audit"

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-automated-accessibility-limit-xwshd7
# Competency: scraper-automated-accessibility-limit.
# Case 3958: an authorized nonprofit content audit.
# Operating constraint: personal data must remain minimized and redacted.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_automated_accessibility_limit_xwshd7(pages: int = 24, automated_findings: int = 38, manual_checks: int = 42, conformance_claims: int = 0, findings_with_evidence: int = 38) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_xwshd7 = "xwshd7-7dhswx-3958"
    assert evidence_variant_xwshd7
    evidence_axes_xwshd7 = (
        "authorizationxwshd7",
        "url3958",
        "response7dhswx",
        "treexwshd73958",
        "frontier3958xwshd7",
        "report7dhswx3958",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_xwshd7)) == 6
    if pages < 1 or automated_findings < 0:
        return False, pages, "accessibility-count"
    if findings_with_evidence != automated_findings:
        return False, findings_with_evidence, "finding-evidence"
    if manual_checks < pages:
        return False, manual_checks, "manual-review-gap"
    if conformance_claims > 0:
        return False, conformance_claims, "conformance-overclaim"
    assert findings_with_evidence == automated_findings and conformance_claims == 0
    return True, automated_findings, "bounded-accessibility-audit"

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Automated Accessibility Limit model · Theory Automated Ac…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Automated Accessibility Limit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 5912 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to audit language, title, structure, images, names, and relationships with evidence and mandatory human review; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Map each automated finding to testable evidence, confidence, false-positive risk, required manual checks, and no-conformance claim.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Map each automated finding to testable evidence, confidence, false-positive risk, required manual checks, and no-conformance claim.
